reasoning: The 30-year fixed jumbo mortgage index is a volatile daily series that roughly follows 10-year Treasury yields. On Jan 16, 2026 (the most recent trading day prior to Jan 18), the value was 6.34. The recent local mean (Jan 2 to Jan 16) is around 6.37. Because the current value is slightly below the local average, mean reversion suggests a marginally higher probability (>50%) that it will increase over the short term (Jan 25). The FRED linear trend estimate is 51% (ignoring the flawed seasonal estimate which wrongly compares 2025's ~7% rates against the absolute threshold of 6.34). For the short horizon of Jan 25, I estimate a 55% chance it increases from 6.34. For Feb 17, macro forecasts for 2026 generally indicate rates hovering with a slight bias toward 'modest declines'. Over this longer horizon, the mean reversion effect weakens, and the macro drift brings the probability closer to 50%. As instructed, my uncertainty increases for the later date.
